comments.author: GridGazer
comments.text: Honestly, the funniest stat in this whole piece is that Red Bull's last drivers' champion is now running Ferrari. Randy Belacik walks out of the cockpit and immediately becomes the most successful thing about RB's recent history. VCARB is the real senior team and everyone knows it.

replies.author: SbinnalaEnjoyer
replies.text: Red Bull being their own junior team. At least they are consistent.

replies.author: fastboi_88
replies.text: Jason Roy reading this article like "wait, I thought I got promoted?"

comments.author: TifosiForever
comments.text: That Cachinho paragraph is brutal. "Most uncomfortable open question heading into the year" - the guy has not turned a wheel yet and they are already measuring the gap left by a 226-point driver. Belacik might be the only TP who would say that out loud. If Cachinho scores under 100 points this season the pressure is going to be unbearable.

replies.author: SilverArrowsFan
replies.text: He got the seat because he filled in for Väisänen mid-2024, now he is replacing Dela Cruz who was runner-up. That is a wild career trajectory.

replies.author: Mekola79
replies.text: Ferrari's F2 academy better be ready. Rivington and Olmedo could be in that seat by summer if Cachinho cannot close the gap to Eetu.

comments.author: PaddockInsider
comments.text: Can we talk about Alpine running Ford-badged Red Bull engines with the full Red Bull logo on the engine cover? That is not a commercial partnership, that is an identity crisis. "Long defined by its French manufacturer identity" - they have got a Greek driver, a half-known German rookie, and a can of Red Bull on the livery. Enstone is having an existential meltdown.

replies.author: on_the_throttle
replies.text: At least Michiii MaraDöner's name sounds like a 3am kebab order. That is the most Alpine thing ever.

replies.author: MaraDönerFan42
replies.text: Put some respect on it - he scored 43 points in that tractor last year and looked decent late on. Pairing him with Kondra is a gamble though. Nick Beier outscored him and lost his seat, make it make sense.

comments.author: DNF_Fakt
comments.text: Sam Fakt "tendency to accumulate avoidable DNFs is the central question mark hanging over his 2026" - they really wrote that with a straight face after a season where Springfield dragged that Aston to 92 points. I would bet on her being the one to watch if the Honda PU works out.

replies.author: SpringfieldStan
replies.text: She has been underrated for two years. 125 points in 2024, 92 in 2025 in a midfield car. Give her a podium-capable machine and she will deliver. Fakt just needs to stop crashing.

comments.author: WrightBackWhereIBelong
comments.text: VCARB lineup is disgusting. Kevu and Wright in the car that has won the last two drivers' titles, while Red Bull tries to figure out if Roy can outscore them. Wright going back after scoring 163 points at RB and still feeling like a step down says everything. The championship goes through that garage, again.

replies.author: StatsNerd_26
replies.text: Fun fact: Kevu's 259 points in 2025 is actually lower than Wright's 264 in 2024. If the car is as dominant this year, the teammate battle alone could be title-deciding.

replies.author: Roy2RB4ever
replies.text: Jason Roy might finish behind both of them in a "worse" car and Red Bull PR will still call it a successful season.

comments.author: WilliamsFaithful
comments.text: Still not over Jack TEF retiring from driving after a 201-point, P3 in the championship season. Man literally said "I could keep scoring podiums but I would rather run the team." Absolute legend. Now we have got Aygen (very good) and a total unknown in Koopman. The 2025 Williams was a rocketship at times - I hope the car is not wasted this year.

replies.author: TEF_era
replies.text: If Aygen scores anywhere near 186 again, we are fine. But that internal gap TEF left is huge. Koopman is either a hidden gem or a placeholder.

comments.author: F2Scout
comments.text: The F2 section buried the lede: Tim Mittens is a real person racing for Hitech and is a Haas academy driver. I am cheering for him based on name alone. Also Mercedes having four juniors (Park, Thompson, Batt, Price) is a flex - they are building a factory line.

replies.author: CatmanCarlo
replies.text: Korv Batt and Andrew Price at Prema is a terrifying combo. Prema is going to farm podiums again. And Jaromir Grunt has serious hype, Red Bull might have found their next VCARB graduate.

replies.author: BroccoliRacing
replies.text: I am sorry but Audi having a driver named Brock Lee is the most Audi thing ever. "Charles Mounir, Brock Lee, and Marek Dubišar" sounds like a firm of accountants.

comments.author: Zwart_Horse_187
comments.text: Maikel Zwart going from Williams 4th in the championship to Mercedes is the move of the winter. 187 points in a car that was not supposed to fight for wins. But Vandesteene is a total question mark - his 34 points in a partial campaign does not scream "ready to partner a front-runner". If Mercedes starts slow, people will say they should have kept Aygen.

replies.author: MercEngineer
replies.text: CMDesigns is not stupid. Vandesteene must have shown something special behind the scenes, because on paper this lineup is high-risk. The Mercedes PU needs to be bulletproof from Melbourne.

comments.author: QuarterPounder
comments.text: Quarter Kevu's name always makes me think he is only 25% of a driver, but the stats say he is 100% champion. Defending the crown from the same seat he won it in, with the 2024 champ as his teammate - if they do not crash into each other, nobody else is touching that constructors' lead.

replies.author: Kevu2Wright
replies.text: VCARB's biggest enemy is VCARB. Two number-one drivers in the fastest car, what could go wrong?

comments.author: HondaHypeTrain
comments.text: Aston Martin ditching Mercedes PU for a brand new Honda unit is the quietest seismic shift on the grid. If that engine is even 95% as good as the Ford/Red Bull PU, Springfield and Fakt could be regular podium visitors. The 2024 car with the Mercedes was a points machine, so this is a legitimate bet.

comments.author: GridGazer
comments.text: One final note: the article says Melbourne will not decide the championship, but it will tell us which narratives have substance. That is a classy way of saying "if Cachinho qualifies P16 and bins it in Q2, Ferrari Twitter is going to be a warzone."

replies.author: MelbourneMadness
replies.text: Albert Park always delivers chaos. I just want to see if the Red Bull branding on the Alpine actually makes it faster or just funnier.
